pi_items.source: Verified through task.json (line 17), setup_task.sh (line 24), and export_result.sh (line 11). User 'ga' is referenced consistently throughout all shell scripts as the OpenICE user.

Verified through task.json metadata (line 18), description states 'Create 3 data pairs' (line 4), setup_task.sh processes samples 1-3 (export_result.sh lines 19-59), and verifier.py checks if valid_pairs >= 3 (line 125).

Verified through task.json metadata (line 19). Consistency check: OCR training datasets typically require images between 50-10000 pixels (verified via web search). 400 pixels is within standard OCR ranges. Verifier.py uses this as upper bound at line 92: 'if 20 < width < max_dim and 20 < height < max_dim'. Description states images must be 'significantly smaller than the full screen' which is typically 1920x1080, and 400 pixels is much smaller than screen width.

Verified through task.json metadata (line 20). Used consistently in verifier.py line 92 as lower bound. Web search shows typical OCR minimum text height is 12 pixels for standard resolution images. 20 pixels is conservative lower bound, slightly more permissive than typical OCR requirements.

Web search confirms OpenICE supports simulated devices including multiparameter monitors.

pi_items.metadata_value: Heart Rate (HR) number OCR training data
pi_items.verified_value: Heart Rate (HR) number OCR training data
pi_items.source: Verified through task.json title and description (line 2, 4). Description states 'Create a labeled computer vision dataset for Heart Rate OCR training' and 'Observe the green Heart Rate (HR) number on the device display'. Web search confirms heart rate is typically displayed as a number on medical monitors in beats per minute (bpm).

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating a synthetic OCR training dataset consisting of 3 labeled image-text pairs. The dataset directory is at /home/ga/Desktop/ocr_data. Each pair contains a heart rate number extracted from a simulated medical device (Multiparameter Monitor in OpenICE), with cropped images (named hr_sample_1/2/3.png) dimensioned between 20-400 pixels and corresponding text labels (hr_sample_1/2/3.txt) containing only the integer heart rate value. This is validated through verifier.py which checks file existence, image dimensions, and semantic correctness of labels via VLM analysis.
